skill.name: xlsx
skill.description: Create, edit, analyze, format, and recalculate spreadsheet files with guidance for formulas, financial models, pandas, openpyxl, and LibreOffice.
skill.summary: Work with XLSX spreadsheets, formulas, formatting, analysis, and recalculation workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings were partly confirmed: recalc.py runs LibreOffice and writes a persistent LibreOffice macro in the user configuration directory. The network, C2 keyword, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and SKILL.md backtick findings are false positives from license links, XML namespaces, Markdown examples, formulas, and documentation text. No evidence found of malicious intent, prompt injection, credential access, or data exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: LibreOffice Subprocess Execution On User Files
medium_findings.description: recalc.py invokes soffice and timeout tools through subprocess.run to recalculate spreadsheet formulas. The command uses argument lists rather than a shell, which lowers injection risk, but it still opens user-provided spreadsheet files in LibreOffice headless mode.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess.run calls are real executable behavior and are required by the recalculation workflow. The absence of shell=True and the use of list arguments reduce command injection risk.

medium_findings.title: Persistent LibreOffice Macro Written To User Configuration
medium_findings.description: The helper creates a LibreOffice macro directory under the user home configuration path and writes Module1.xba. This persistent user-level change is legitimate for formula recalculation but should be disclosed because it modifies application configuration outside the current project.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code explicitly constructs a hidden LibreOffice configuration path and writes Module1.xba there. The macro content is visible and limited to recalculation and saving, so malicious intent is not confirmed.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Findings Are Documentation Or XML Identifiers
low_findings.description: The LICENSE.txt URLs are legal reference links, and the recalc.py URL is an OpenOffice XML namespace inside macro markup. I did not find evidence of runtime network access or data transmission.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited URLs are static text and XML metadata, not code that performs requests. No network API call is present at these locations.

low_findings.title: SKILL.md Command And Reconnaissance Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md detections occur in Markdown instructions and code examples for pandas, openpyxl, and recalc.py usage. They are not executed by the skill file itself, but users should still review generated code before running it on sensitive files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious patterns are inside Markdown prose or fenced examples. They describe expected spreadsheet workflows rather than hidden executable behavior.

low_findings.title: C2 And Weak Cryptography Keywords Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The C2 finding maps to an Excel cell reference formula, and weak cryptography hits map to words such as Markdown, modify, or model context. No evidence found of command-and-control behavior or cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited context is spreadsheet documentation and formula examples. I did not find cryptographic code, encoded payloads, callbacks, or remote-control logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Process Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The recalculation helper starts external LibreOffice and timeout binaries. This is a powerful pattern even though it is used for a clear spreadsheet purpose.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is confirmed by direct subprocess.run calls. The risk is moderated because commands are passed as lists and no shell execution is used.

dangerous_patterns.title: Home Directory Configuration Write
dangerous_patterns.description: The script writes a LibreOffice macro file under a hidden user configuration path. This changes persistent application state outside the spreadsheet file.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path and file write are explicit in the code. The written macro is visible and scoped to recalculation, which reduces concern about hidden behavior.

content.value_statement: Spreadsheet work often fails when formulas, formatting, and source data are handled separately. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through creating, editing, analyzing, and recalculating XLSX files.
actual_capabilities: Creates new XLSX workbooks with sheets, formulas, formatting, and column sizing.
Edits existing spreadsheets while preserving formulas and workbook structure.
Uses pandas guidance for reading, analyzing, and exporting tabular spreadsheet data.
Uses openpyxl guidance for formulas, formatting, sheet edits, and workbook inspection.
Runs a provided LibreOffice-based helper to recalculate formulas and report spreadsheet errors.
Provides financial model conventions for color coding, number formats, assumptions, and source notes.
limitations: Formula recalculation requires LibreOffice and may modify the user LibreOffice configuration.
The helper opens spreadsheet files with external software, so untrusted files need caution.
The skill gives workflow guidance and examples; it does not replace manual review of important models.
Very large workbooks may require read-only or write-only processing to avoid memory issues.

use_cases.title: Build A Financial Model
use_cases.description: Create a workbook with assumptions, formulas, formatting, source notes, and recalculated outputs.

use_cases.title: Clean Spreadsheet Data
use_cases.description: Load Excel data, inspect columns, summarize values, and export a clean workbook for review.

use_cases.title: Update An Existing Template
use_cases.description: Modify rows, formulas, and sheets while preserving the template style and existing workbook conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect Workbook Data
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an uploaded spreadsheet
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this spreadsheet and summarize its sheets, key columns, data quality issues, and any formulas that need attention.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Formatted Workbook
prompt_templates.scenario: Build a new spreadsheet from structured requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an XLSX workbook with separate input, calculation, and summary sheets. Use formulas for all calculations and apply clear number formats.

prompt_templates.title: Update A Template Safely
prompt_templates.scenario: Modify an existing workbook without breaking formulas
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this workbook with the new data while preserving existing formatting, formulas, sheet names, and template conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Validate A Financial Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this financial model for formula errors, broken references, inconsistent formatting, hardcoded calculations, and missing source documentation.

output_examples.input: A sales dataset with monthly revenue, costs, and region fields.
output_examples.output: A workbook with clean data, regional summaries, monthly trend formulas, charts, and a short list of validation notes.

output_examples.input: An existing budget template that needs updated assumptions and new forecast months.
output_examples.output: An updated template with preserved styling, extended formulas, refreshed assumptions, and no reported formula errors.

output_examples.input: A financial model with broken references and inconsistent number formats.
output_examples.output: A review summary listing affected sheets, formula error locations, formatting fixes, and recommended model corrections.

best_practices: Keep assumptions in dedicated cells and reference them from formulas.
Recalculate formula workbooks and review reported errors before delivery.
Match existing workbook formatting when editing templates.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode calculated results that should remain dynamic formulas.
Do not save a workbook loaded with calculated values only if formulas must be preserved.
Do not run recalculation helpers on untrusted spreadsheets without reviewing the file source.

faq.question: Can this skill create new Excel files?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for creating XLSX files with data, formulas, formatting, sheets, and column sizing.

faq.question: Can it edit existing workbooks?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes preserving existing formulas, formatting, sheet structure, and template conventions during edits.

faq.question: Does formula recalculation require extra software?
faq.answer: Yes. The provided recalculation helper requires LibreOffice and starts it in headless mode.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive spreadsheets?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill has no evidence of exfiltration, but the helper opens files with external software and writes LibreOffice configuration.

faq.question: What libraries does the skill recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends pandas for data analysis and openpyxl for workbook structure, formulas, and formatting.

faq.question: Can it help with financial model formatting?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes conventions for input colors, formulas, links, number formats, assumptions, and source documentation.
